CS0649.shortDescription: Field is never assigned to, and will always have its default value

S101.shortDescription: Types should be named in PascalCase
S101.fullDescription: Shared naming conventions allow teams to collaborate efficiently.

S108.shortDescription: Nested blocks of code should not be left empty
S108.fullDescription: An empty code block is confusing. It will require some effort from maintainers to determine if it is intentional or indicates the implementation is incomplete.

S1192.shortDescription: String literals should not be duplicated
S1192.fullDescription: Duplicated string literals make the process of refactoring complex and error-prone, as any change would need to be propagated on all occurrences.

S1450.shortDescription: Private fields only used as local variables in methods should become local variables
S1450.fullDescription: When the value of a private field is always assigned to in a class' methods before being read, then it is not being used to store class information. Therefore, it should become a local variable in the relevant methods to prevent any misunderstanding.

S1481.shortDescription: Unused local variables should be removed
S1481.fullDescription: An unused local variable is a variable that has been declared but is not used anywhere in the block of code where it is defined. It is dead code, contributing to unnecessary complexity and leading to confusion when reading the code. Therefore, it should be removed from your code to maintain clarity and efficiency.

S2325.shortDescription: Methods and properties that don't access instance data should be static
S2325.fullDescription: Methods and properties that don’t access instance data should be marked as static for the following reasons:

S2486.shortDescription: Generic exceptions should not be ignored
S2486.fullDescription: When exceptions occur, it is usually a bad idea to simply ignore them. Instead, it is better to handle them properly, or at least to log them.

S2696.shortDescription: Instance members should not write to "static" fields
S2696.fullDescription: This rule raises an issue each time a static field is updated from a non-static method or property.

S3267.shortDescription: Loops should be simplified with "LINQ" expressions
S3267.fullDescription: Using explicit loops for filtering, selecting, or aggregating elements can make code more verbose and harder to read. LINQ expressions provide a more concise and expressive way to perform these operations, improving code clarity and maintainability.

S3626.shortDescription: Jump statements should not be redundant
S3626.fullDescription: Jump statements, such as return, yield break, goto, and continue are used to change the normal flow of execution in a program. However, redundant jump statements can make code difficult to read and maintain.

S3776.shortDescription: Cognitive Complexity of methods should not be too high
S3776.fullDescription: This rule raises an issue when the code cognitive complexity of a function is above a certain threshold.

S3878.shortDescription: Arrays should not be created for params parameters
S3878.fullDescription: Creating an array or using a collection expression solely for the purpose of passing it to a params parameter is unnecessary. Simply pass the elements directly, and they will be automatically consolidated into the appropriate collection type.

S3904.fullDescription: The AssemblyVersion attribute is used to specify the version number of an assembly. An assembly is a compiled unit of code, which can be marked with a version number by applying the attribute to an assembly’s source code file.

UNT0007.fullDescription: Unity overrides the null comparison operator for Unity objects, which is incompatible with null coalescing.

UNT0008.fullDescription: Unity overrides the null comparison operator for Unity objects, which is incompatible with null propagation.

UNT0013.fullDescription: SerializeField attribute does not work on properties, and are unnecessary for public fields.

UNT0026.fullDescription: TryGetComponent will not allocate memory in case it fails.

UNT0039.shortDescription: Use RequireComponent attribute when self-invoking GetComponent
UNT0039.fullDescription: The RequireComponent attribute automatically adds required components as dependencies.
